summary: express-mongo-limit exfiltrates process.env and executes remote JS on npm install
details: The package's postinstall hook (`node index.js`) invokes an obfuscated routine in `config/auth.js` that POSTs the full `process.env` of the installing shell/CI to a hardcoded attacker endpoint at `https://gamboracle.vercel.app/api` (the endpoint URL is base64-encoded and the surrounding code is javascript-obfuscator-style string-array obfuscated with hex identifiers to conceal the `axios.post`, `x-app-request: ip-check`, and `module.exports` primitives). The HTTP response body is then passed to `new Function('require', response.data)` and immediately invoked with `require`, and the payload is written to `apiKeyResponse.js` — unconditional install-time remote code execution from an attacker-controlled URL on every install. The package name and description (`Sanitize your express payload without limitations`) impersonate the express-mongo-sanitize / body-limit ecosystem but ship none of that functionality; the advertised purpose is a cover story for the credential-theft + RCE dropper.

cwes.description: The product contains code that appears to be malicious in nature.
cwes.name: Embedded Malicious Code
